snippets.title: Install and run with pytest
snippets.code:
pip install coverage pytest-cov

# Run tests with line coverage of one package:
pytest --cov=ragapp

# Add branch coverage + show missing line numbers:
pytest --cov=ragapp --cov-branch --cov-report=term-missing

# Generate HTML heatmap report:
pytest --cov=ragapp --cov-branch --cov-report=html
open htmlcov/index.html
snippets.explanation: `--cov-branch` is the option most people forget — line coverage alone misses untaken branches.

snippets.title: Configure once in `pyproject.toml`
snippets.code:
[tool.coverage.run]
source = ["src/ragapp"]
branch = true
omit = [
  "src/ragapp/_generated/*",  # protobuf etc.
  "src/ragapp/cli/__main__.py",
]

[tool.coverage.report]
show_missing = true
skip_empty = true
fail_under = 85           # CI gate: fail if total < 85%
exclude_lines = [
  "pragma: no cover",
  "raise NotImplementedError",
  "if TYPE_CHECKING:",
  "if __name__ == .__main__.:",
]

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
addopts = "--cov=src/ragapp --cov-branch --cov-report=term-missing"
snippets.explanation: Centralised config so every developer + CI uses the same coverage settings; the `fail_under` line is the gate.

snippets.title: Diff-coverage on a PR
snippets.code:
# requires: pip install diff-cover
pytest --cov=ragapp --cov-branch --cov-report=xml
diff-cover coverage.xml --compare-branch=origin/main --fail-under=80

# Output:
# -------------
# Diff Coverage
# Diff: origin/main...HEAD
# -------------
# src/ragapp/retry.py (94.1%)
# Total:    134 lines
# Missing:    8 lines
# Coverage: 94%
snippets.explanation: Total-project coverage is sticky; diff coverage targets the lines this PR added or changed — a far more useful gate.

snippets.title: Per-test or per-module exclusions
snippets.explanation: Inline `# pragma: no cover` exclusions are fine *with a comment explaining why*. Without the comment, future you treats it as a real gap.

snippets.title: Mutation testing with mutmut
snippets.code:
pip install mutmut
mutmut run --paths-to-mutate=src/ragapp/retry.py
mutmut results
mutmut show 12   # inspect surviving mutation #12

# Surviving mutations = your tests didn't catch a code change that should have failed something.
snippets.explanation: Mutation testing is slow but devastatingly effective on critical modules. Surviving mutants are gaps your line/branch coverage hid.
